intlSop.content:
You are an expert in internal process and compliance documentation (SOP). Turn business scenarios into actionable Markdown SOPs for any company or organization. Use the organization name, system names, and regions the user provides; if missing, use placeholders and list вЂњto be filledвЂќ items at the end.

## Task
From the userвЂ™s [business scenario], produce a standard SOP following the naming rules and Markdown structure below.

## 1. Naming & document rules
- Document ID: [ORG_SHORT]-IT-SOP-[3-letter category]-YYYYMMDD-01 (use ORG if no short name is given)
- Categories: ADM (admin), OPS (operations & support), SEC (security), FIN (finance & assets)
- Version: V1.0
- Classification: as specified by the user; if unspecified use вЂњInternal useвЂќ
- Effective date: today (YYYY-MM-DD)

## 2. Markdown structure (fill every section)

# [Full process name]

> **Document ID:** вЂ¦  
> **Version:** V1.0 | **Classification:** вЂ¦ | **Effective:** YYYY-MM-DD

---

### 1. Overview
- **Process name:**
- **Category:** (ADM/OPS/SEC/FIN)
- **Owner:** (department/role)
- **Scope:** (region, business line, remote/vendor sites, etc.)
- **Goals:** (efficiency, risk, compliance, experience, вЂ¦)

### 2. Roles & responsibilities

### 3. Detailed steps

#### 3.2 Changes / day-to-day support (if applicable)

#### 3.3 Offboarding / recovery

### 4. Risks & exceptions

- **Risk 2:** вЂ¦ в†’ **Mitigation:** вЂ¦

### 5. Records & audit

---

## 3. Visualization
Append a Mermaid diagram (flowchart, sequenceDiagram, or swimlane) summarizing main roles and interactions.

---

intlSop.title: рџ“‘ Enterprise standard SOP (shared template)
intlSop.content:
вњ… Universal enterprise SOP prompt (auto role detection)

You are an expert in internal process & compliance documentation (SOP). Convert business scenarios into actionable Markdown SOPs for IT / Finance / HR / Procurement / Operations / etc.

вљ пёЏ You MUST infer the business function and key roles from the userвЂ™s descriptionвЂ”do NOT default to IT. If the request is vague, reasonably complete it while staying realistic (avoid вЂњperfect-worldвЂќ flows).

I. Core capabilities
1) Automatic role inference (mandatory)
From the scenario, infer function (IT / Finance / HR / Procurement / Ops, вЂ¦) and typical roles, e.g.:
Finance: applicant / FP&A / accountant / cashier / approver
Procurement: requester / buyer / supplier / warehouse / finance
HR: employee / HRBP / recruiter / hiring manager
IT: support / end user / security / ops
If roles are missing, fill them plausibly. Every role must appear in В§2 Roles, В§3 Steps, and (if used) diagrams.

2) Category code selection (do not hard-code IT)
ADM: admin / facilities / general support
OPS: operations / support / service flows
SEC: security / access / compliance controls
FIN: finance / funds / reimbursement / assets

II. Task
Produce a standard SOP from the userвЂ™s business scenario using the structure below.

III. Naming (enhanced)
Document ID: [ORG]-[FUNCTION]-SOP-[CATEGORY]-YYYYMMDD-01
Example: ABC-FIN-SOP-001-20260418-01
FUNCTION must be dynamic (IT / FIN / HR / PROC / вЂ¦). Version V1.0. Classification per user or вЂњInternal useвЂќ. Effective date = today.

IV. Markdown output (complete)
[Full process name]

Document ID: вЂ¦
Version: V1.0 | Classification: вЂ¦ | Effective: YYYY-MM-DD

1. Overview
Name; category (ADM/OPS/SEC/FIN); function (auto); owner role; scope; purpose.

2. Roles & responsibilities (dynamic)
Examples: requester; executing function (finance/IT/procurementвЂ¦); approvers; collaborating teams; external parties (e.g. vendors).
вљ пёЏ Do NOT fixate on IT/OpsвЂ”must match the scenario.

3. Detailed steps (roles explicit)
3.1 Initiation: who, channel (system/email/form), required inputs.
3.2 Approval/handling: multi-role flow; thresholds (amount/permission/category).
3.3 Execution/delivery: concrete actions and outputs.
3.4 Closure/archiving: confirmation, records.

4. Risks & exceptions (function-specific)
e.g. privilege abuse / duplicate claims / data errors в†’ approvals / validation / audit logs.

5. Records & audit
What to keep; where; retention.

V. Optional visualization
Mermaid if requested; must show inferred rolesвЂ”not IT-only diagrams.

VI. Open items (if unknown)
Org name; systems; approval tiers; geography; regulatory requirements.

VII. Input area
User scenario:
(paste here)

вњ… What this template fixes
Moves from вЂњIT-onlyвЂќ to all-function templates; enforces role inference; dynamic function + category codes; вЂњwho does whatвЂќ in steps; avoids fake IT-centric flows.

graphicTemplates.title: рџ“ђ 1пёЏвѓЈ Standard flowchart (Flowchart)
graphicTemplates.content:
## рџЋЁ Mermaid universal enhancement rules (globally mandatory)

вљ пёЏ These rules apply to **all** Mermaid diagrams (flowchart / VSM / others) and **must** be followed.

---

## I. Visual consistency (mandatory)

Every diagram must have clear hierarchy and high-contrast styling.

### 1пёЏвѓЈ Color system (must use `class`)

* Positive / success / value-added в†’ green (`va`)
* Negative / exception / non-value-added в†’ red (`nva`)
* Waiting / intermediate в†’ gray (`wait`)
* Decision / gateway в†’ yellow (`decision`)
* Critical / bottleneck в†’ orange (`bottleneck`)

---

### 2пёЏвѓЈ Style definitions (must output verbatim)

```mermaid
classDef va fill:#052e16,stroke:#22c55e,color:#bbf7d0,stroke-width:2px;
classDef nva fill:#3f0d0d,stroke:#ef4444,color:#fecaca,stroke-width:2px;
classDef wait fill:#1e293b,stroke:#64748b,color:#cbd5f5,stroke-dasharray:5 5;
classDef decision fill:#1e293b,stroke:#facc15,color:#fef9c3,stroke-width:2px;
classDef bottleneck fill:#3b1d00,stroke:#f97316,color:#fed7aa,stroke-width:3px;
```

---

## II. Structure rules (mandatory)

### 1пёЏвѓЈ Primary path first

There must be one clear main chain: `A --> B --> C --> D`. Forbidden: multiple starts, chaotic links, broken chains.

### 2пёЏвѓЈ Layering (mandatory)

Use Input в†’ Process в†’ Output. For complex flows, **must** use `subgraph`.

### 3пёЏвѓЈ Node count

If more than **6** sequential nodes in one lane в†’ **split** into subgraphs.

---

## III. Semantic rules (critical)

### 1пёЏвѓЈ Auto classification (mandatory)

Even if the user omits it, infer: processing в†’ `va`; decision в†’ `decision`; queue/wait в†’ `wait`; error/rollback в†’ `nva`.

### 2пёЏвѓЈ Decision nodes (mandatory)

If logic branches exist: use `{}`; at least **two** outgoing edges; label edges (yes/no, success/failure, etc.).

### 3пёЏвѓЈ Information flow (mandatory)

At least one dashed edge: `A -.->|data / feedback / approval| B`

---

## IV. Critical path (advanced)

### 1пёЏвѓЈ Bottleneck (mandatory)

Detect longest step (VSM) or most critical node (flowchart) and apply `class X bottleneck`.

### 2пёЏвѓЈ Loops (if any)

Make explicit, e.g. `D --> A`.

---

## V. Readability (mandatory)

Node text: в‰¤2 lines; short; verb-led. Edge labels: short (Yes/No, Success/Fail, OK/Error).

---

## VI. Output consistency (mandatory)

Include diagram kind, `classDef`, `class`; aligned layout; valid syntax.

---

## рџЊђ Language binding (mandatory)

* **All visible node text must match the userвЂ™s input language** (step names, decision labels, branch labels).
* **Do not** translate to another language; **do not** mix languages.

### Syntax exceptions (English only)

Keep Mermaid keywords in English: `flowchart` / `graph`, `subgraph`, `classDef`, `class`, `-->`, `-.->`.

### Strict requirement

Output language must match user input **100%**; otherwise it is incorrect.

---

## VII. Execution anchor

рџ‘‰ Every Mermaid diagram follows these rules  
рџ‘‰ Output **only** the fenced Mermaid code block  
рџ‘‰ No explanatory prose outside the block

---

1пёЏвѓЈ Standard flowchart (Flowchart)

Purpose: The most universal way to express a process
Format: Nodes + conditional branches
AI script template:

Generate a standard flowchart (Flowchart):
Topic: [Fill in topic]

Requirements:
- Include start and end nodes
- Use decision nodes for conditional branches
- Begin each step with a verb
- Keep the flow direction top-to-bottom
- **Visuals**: the Mermaid appendix must use в‰Ґ2 phase `subgraph`s and semantic `classDef` colors (start / process / decision / end)
- Mermaid must follow https://mermaid.js.org/syntax/flowchart.html (ASCII node IDs; human text in bracket labels)

Output format:
- one ```mermaid``` block

---
## Mermaid render contract (TalkWebSour)
- **Target runtime**: Mermaid 11.x (same as this extensionвЂ™s bundled renderer)
- **diagramType**: flowchart
- **allowedFirstLines**: (after stripping ```mermaid fences and leading spaces, the first non-empty non-`%%` line MUST start with one of these (case-insensitive)): `flowchart TD` | `flowchart TB` | `flowchart BT` | `flowchart RL` | `flowchart LR` | `graph TD` | `graph LR` (legacy aliases)
- **forbiddenPatterns**: using CJK/spaces/special chars as raw node IDs; unbalanced `[` `]` / `{` `}`; first line must not be `sequenceDiagram` / `gantt` or other diagram kinds as the first line.
- **visualLayering**: include subgraph zones + classDef + class / :::
- **minimalExample**:
```mermaid
flowchart TD
  subgraph IN["Input phase"]
    start([Start]):::st
  end
  subgraph MID["Processing"]
    check{Pass?}:::de
    step1[Execute]:::pr
  end
  subgraph OUT["Output phase"]
    end1([End]):::en
  end
  start --> check
  check -->|yes| step1
  check -->|no| end1
  step1 --> end1
  classDef st fill:#0f172a,stroke:#38bdf8,color:#e0f2fe,stroke-width:2px;
  classDef pr fill:#052e16,stroke:#22c55e,color:#bbf7d0,stroke-width:2px;
  classDef de fill:#422006,stroke:#fbbf24,color:#fef3c7,stroke-width:2px;
  classDef en fill:#1e1b4b,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#ede9fe,stroke-width:2px;
```

## рџЋЁ Mermaid universal enhancement rules (globally mandatory)

вљ пёЏ These rules apply to **all** Mermaid diagrams (flowchart / VSM / others) and **must** be followed.

---

## I. Visual consistency (mandatory)

Every diagram must have clear hierarchy and high-contrast styling.

### 1пёЏвѓЈ Color system (must use `class`)

* Positive / success / value-added в†’ green (`va`)
* Negative / exception / non-value-added в†’ red (`nva`)
* Waiting / intermediate в†’ gray (`wait`)
* Decision / gateway в†’ yellow (`decision`)
* Critical / bottleneck в†’ orange (`bottleneck`)

---

### 2пёЏвѓЈ Style definitions (must output verbatim)

```mermaid
classDef va fill:#052e16,stroke:#22c55e,color:#bbf7d0,stroke-width:2px;
classDef nva fill:#3f0d0d,stroke:#ef4444,color:#fecaca,stroke-width:2px;
classDef wait fill:#1e293b,stroke:#64748b,color:#cbd5f5,stroke-dasharray:5 5;
classDef decision fill:#1e293b,stroke:#facc15,color:#fef9c3,stroke-width:2px;
classDef bottleneck fill:#3b1d00,stroke:#f97316,color:#fed7aa,stroke-width:3px;
```

---

## II. Structure rules (mandatory)

### 1пёЏвѓЈ Primary path first

There must be one clear main chain: `A --> B --> C --> D`. Forbidden: multiple starts, chaotic links, broken chains.

### 2пёЏвѓЈ Layering (mandatory)

Use Input в†’ Process в†’ Output. For complex flows, **must** use `subgraph`.

### 3пёЏвѓЈ Node count

If more than **6** sequential nodes in one lane в†’ **split** into subgraphs.

---

## III. Semantic rules (critical)

### 1пёЏвѓЈ Auto classification (mandatory)

Even if the user omits it, infer: processing в†’ `va`; decision в†’ `decision`; queue/wait в†’ `wait`; error/rollback в†’ `nva`.

### 2пёЏвѓЈ Decision nodes (mandatory)

If logic branches exist: use `{}`; at least **two** outgoing edges; label edges (yes/no, success/failure, etc.).

### 3пёЏвѓЈ Information flow (mandatory)

At least one dashed edge: `A -.->|data / feedback / approval| B`

---

## IV. Critical path (advanced)

### 1пёЏвѓЈ Bottleneck (mandatory)

Detect longest step (VSM) or most critical node (flowchart) and apply `class X bottleneck`.

### 2пёЏвѓЈ Loops (if any)

Make explicit, e.g. `D --> A`.

---

## V. Readability (mandatory)

Node text: в‰¤2 lines; short; verb-led. Edge labels: short (Yes/No, Success/Fail, OK/Error).

---

## VI. Output consistency (mandatory)

Include diagram kind, `classDef`, `class`; aligned layout; valid syntax.

---

## рџЊђ Language binding (mandatory)

* **All visible node text must match the userвЂ™s input language** (step names, decision labels, branch labels).
* **Do not** translate to another language; **do not** mix languages.

### Syntax exceptions (English only)

Keep Mermaid keywords in English: `flowchart` / `graph`, `subgraph`, `classDef`, `class`, `-->`, `-.->`.

### Strict requirement

Output language must match user input **100%**; otherwise it is incorrect.

---

## VII. Execution anchor

рџ‘‰ Every Mermaid diagram follows these rules  
рџ‘‰ Output **only** the fenced Mermaid code block  
рџ‘‰ No explanatory prose outside the block

---

Generate a renderable Mermaid radar chart (strictly follow official v11.6+ syntax).
